info.title: Friendli Suite API Reference
info.description: This is an OpenAPI reference of Friendli Suite API.

post.description: Generate a model response from a list of messages comprising a conversation. Compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions API, with support for streaming, tool calls, and structured outputs.

parameters.name: X-Friendli-Team

schema.description: ID of team to run requests as (optional parameter).
schema.title: X-Friendli-Team

parameters.description: ID of team to run requests as (optional parameter).

message.content: Hello there, how may I assist you today?

x-speakeasy-usage-example.description: Generate a model response from a list of messages comprising a conversation. Compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions API, with support for streaming, tool calls, and structured outputs.

metadata.title: Model APIs Chat Completions
metadata.og:title: Model APIs Chat Completions
metadata.description: Generate a model response from a list of messages comprising a conversation. Compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions API, with support for streaming, tool calls, and structured outputs.
metadata.og:description: Generate a model response from a list of messages comprising a conversation. Compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions API, with support for streaming, tool calls, and structured outputs.

x-mint.content:
Generate a model response from a list of messages comprising a conversation. Compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions API, with support for streaming, tool calls, and structured outputs.

See available models at [this pricing table](/guides/model-apis/pricing#billing-methods).

To request successfully, it is mandatory to enter a **Personal API Key** (e.g. flp_XXX) value in the **Bearer Token** field.
Refer to the [authentication section](/openapi/introduction#authentication) on our introduction page to learn how to acquire this variable and [visit here](https://friendli.ai/suite/~/setting/keys) to generate your API Key.

When streaming mode is used (i.e., `stream` option is set to `true`), the response is in MIME type `text/event-stream`. Otherwise, the content type is `application/json`.
You can view the schema of the streamed sequence of chunk objects in streaming mode [here](/openapi/model-apis/chat-completions-chunk-object).

<Tip>You can explore examples on the [Friendli Model APIs](https://friendli.ai/get-started/model-apis) playground and adjust settings with just a few clicks.</Tip>

post.description: Generate a model response from text or image inputs. Follows the OpenAI Responses API format, with support for streaming, function and custom tool calls, structured outputs, and reasoning controls.

schema.description: ID of team to run requests as (optional parameter).
schema.title: X-Friendli-Team

parameters.description: ID of team to run requests as (optional parameter).

content.text: Hello there, how may I assist you today?

metadata.title: Model APIs Responses
metadata.og:title: Model APIs Responses
metadata.description: Generate a model response from text or image inputs. Follows the OpenAI Responses API format, with support for streaming, function and custom tool calls, structured outputs, and reasoning controls.
metadata.og:description: Generate a model response from text or image inputs. Follows the OpenAI Responses API format, with support for streaming, function and custom tool calls, structured outputs, and reasoning controls.

x-mint.content:
Generate a model response from text or image inputs. Follows the OpenAI Responses API format, with support for streaming, function and custom tool calls, structured outputs, and reasoning controls.

See available models at [this pricing table](/guides/model-apis/pricing#billing-methods).

<Note>
The Responses API may not be supported by all models available on Model APIs.
</Note>

To request successfully, it is mandatory to enter a **Personal API Key** (e.g. flp_XXX) value in the **Bearer Token** field.
Refer to the [authentication section](/openapi/introduction#authentication) on our introduction page to learn how to acquire this variable and [visit here](https://friendli.ai/suite/~/setting/keys) to generate your API Key.

When streaming mode is used (i.e., `stream` option is set to `true`), the response is in MIME type `text/event-stream`. Otherwise, the content type is `application/json`.
You can view the schema of the streamed sequence of chunk objects in streaming mode [here](/openapi/model-apis/responses-chunk-object).

<Info>
This API is currently in **Beta**.
While we strive to provide a stable and reliable experience, this feature is still under active development.
As a result, you may encounter unexpected behavior or limitations.
We encourage you to provide feedback to help us improve the feature before its official release.

- [Feature request & feedback](mailto:support@friendli.ai)
- [Contact support](mailto:support@friendli.ai)

</Info>

post.description: Generate a model response from a conversation using the Anthropic Messages API format. Supports streaming, function tool calls, and extended thinking.

parameters.name: X-Friendli-Team

schema.description: ID of team to run requests as (optional parameter).
schema.title: X-Friendli-Team

parameters.description: ID of team to run requests as (optional parameter).

200.description: Successfully generated a Messages-style response. For streaming (`text/event-stream`) event and chunk details, see [Messages chunk object](/openapi/model-apis/messages-chunk-object).

content.text: I can answer questions, generate text, and help with coding tasks.

metadata.title: Model APIs Messages
metadata.og:title: Model APIs Messages
metadata.description: Generate a model response from a conversation using the Anthropic Messages API format. Supports streaming, function tool calls, and extended thinking.
metadata.og:description: Generate a model response from a conversation using the Anthropic Messages API format. Supports streaming, function tool calls, and extended thinking.

x-mint.content:
Generate a model response from a conversation using the Anthropic Messages API format. Supports streaming, function tool calls, and extended thinking.

See available models at [this pricing table](/guides/model-apis/pricing#billing-methods).

<Note>
The Messages API may not be supported by all models available on Model APIs.
</Note>

To request successfully, it is mandatory to enter a **Personal API Key** (e.g. flp_XXX) value in the **Bearer Token** field.
Refer to the [authentication section](/openapi/introduction#authentication) on our introduction page to learn how to acquire this variable and [visit here](https://friendli.ai/suite/~/setting/keys) to generate your API Key.

When streaming mode is used (i.e., `stream` option is set to `true`), the response is in MIME type `text/event-stream`. Otherwise, the content type is `application/json`.
You can view the schema of the streamed sequence of chunk objects in streaming mode [here](/openapi/model-apis/messages-chunk-object).

<Warning>Server-side tools are not supported in the Messages API. In `tools`, only custom/client function tools are used; non-`custom` tool types are ignored.</Warning>

<Info>
This API is currently in **Beta**.
While we strive to provide a stable and reliable experience, this feature is still under active development.
As a result, you may encounter unexpected behavior or limitations.
We encourage you to provide feedback to help us improve the feature before its official release.

- [Feature request & feedback](mailto:support@friendli.ai)
- [Contact support](mailto:support@friendli.ai)

</Info>

model.title: Model
model.description: Code of the model to use. See [available model list](https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/model-apis/pricing#billing-methods).

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far.

chat_template_kwargs.title: Chat Template Kwargs
chat_template_kwargs.description: Additional keyword arguments supplied to the template renderer. These parameters will be available for use within the chat template.

eos_token.title: Eos Token
eos_token.description: A list of endpoint sentence tokens.

frequency_penalty.title: Frequency Penalty
frequency_penalty.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled, taking into account their frequency in the preceding text. This penalization diminishes the model's tendency to reproduce identical lines verbatim.

logit_bias.title: Logit Bias
logit_bias.description: Accepts a JSON object that maps tokens to an associated bias value. Mathematically, the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling. The exact effect will vary per model.

logprobs.title: Logprobs
logprobs.description: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate. For decoder-only models like GPT, the length of your input tokens plus `max_tokens` should not exceed the model's maximum length (e.g., 2048 for OpenAI GPT-3). For encoder-decoder models like T5 or BlenderBot, `max_tokens` should not exceed the model's maximum output length. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`max_new_tokens`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.max_new_tokens) argument.

min_p.title: Min P
min_p.description: A scaling factor used to determine the minimum token probability threshold. This threshold is calculated as `min_p` multiplied by the probability of the most likely token. Tokens with probabilities below this scaled threshold are excluded from sampling. Values range from 0.0 (inclusive) to 1.0 (inclusive). Higher values result in stricter filtering, while lower values allow for greater diversity. The default value of 0.0 disables filtering, allowing all tokens to be considered for sampling.

n.title: N
n.description: The number of independently generated results for the prompt. Defaults to 1. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`num_return_sequences`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.num_return_sequences) argument.

parallel_tool_calls.title: Parallel Tool Calls
parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to enable parallel function calling.

presence_penalty.title: Presence Penalty
presence_penalty.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled at least once in the existing text.

repetition_penalty.title: Repetition Penalty
repetition_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens that have already appeared in the generated result (plus the input tokens for decoder-only models). Should be positive value (1.0 means no penalty). See [keskar et al., 2019](https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.05858) for more details. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`repetition_penalty`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.repetition_penalty) argument.

reasoning_effort.title: Reasoning Effort
reasoning_effort.description: Sets how much reasoning the model does before answering. Higher values give more thorough responses but take longer. This affects reasoning models only, and the available options depend on the model.

reasoning_budget.title: Reasoning Budget
reasoning_budget.description: Specifies a positive integer that defines a limit on the number of tokens used for internal reasoning tokens. This parameter is only effective for reasoning models.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Seed to control random procedure. If nothing is given, random seed is used for sampling, and return the seed along with the generated result. When using the `n` argument, you can pass a list of seed values to control all of the independent generations.

stop.title: Stop
stop.description: When one of the stop phrases appears in the generation result, the API will stop generation. The stop phrases are excluded from the result. Defaults to empty list.

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the generation result. When set to `true`, each token is sent as [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format) once generated.

stream_options.description:
Options related to stream.
It can only be used when `stream: true`.

parse_reasoning.title: Parse Reasoning
parse_reasoning.description:
Parses model reasoning into `reasoning_content` while keeping the answer in `content`. Default value may vary between endpoints.

For more detailed information, please refer [here](https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/reasoning#reasoning-parsing-with-friendli).

include_reasoning.title: Include Reasoning
include_reasoning.description:
When `parse_reasoning=true`, include parsed reasoning (`reasoning_content`). Defaults to true.

For more detailed information, please refer [here](https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/reasoning#reasoning-parsing-with-friendli).

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Smaller temperature makes the generation result closer to greedy, argmax (i.e., `top_k = 1`) sampling. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`temperature`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.temperature) argument.

tool_choice.title: Tool Choice
tool_choice.description:
Determines the tool calling behavior of the model.
When set to `none`, the model will bypass tool execution and generate a response directly.
In `auto` mode (the default), the model dynamically decides whether to call a tool or respond with a message.
Alternatively, setting `required` ensures that the model invokes at least one tool before responding to the user.
You can also specify a particular tool by `{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "my_function"}}`.

top_k.title: Top K
top_k.description: Limits sampling to the top k tokens with the highest probabilities. Values range from 0 (no filtering) to the model's vocabulary size (inclusive). The default value of 0 applies no filtering, allowing all tokens.

top_logprobs.title: Top Logprobs
top_logprobs.description: The number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability. `logprobs` must be set to true if this parameter is used.

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: Keeps only the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probabilities reach `top_p` or higher. Values range from 0.0 (exclusive) to 1.0 (inclusive). The default value of 1.0 includes all tokens, allowing maximum diversity.

xtc_threshold.title: Xtc Threshold
xtc_threshold.description: A probability threshold used to identify “top choice” tokens for exclusion in XTC (Exclude Top Choices) sampling. Tokens with probabilities at or above this threshold are considered viable candidates, and all but the least likely viable token are excluded from sampling. This option reduces the dominance of highly probable tokens while preserving some diversity by keeping the least confident “top choice.” Values range from 0.0 (inclusive) to 1.0 (inclusive). Higher values make the filtering more selective by requiring higher probabilities to trigger exclusion, while lower values apply filtering more broadly. The default value of 0.0 disables XTC filtering entirely.

xtc_probability.title: Xtc Probability
xtc_probability.description: The probability that XTC (Exclude Top Choices) filtering will be applied for each sampling decision. When XTC is triggered, high-probability tokens above the `xtc_threshold` are excluded except for the least likely viable token. This stochastic activation allows for a balance between standard sampling and creativity-boosting exclusion filtering. Values range from 0.0 (inclusive) to 1.0 (inclusive), where 0.0 means XTC is never applied, 1.0 means XTC is always applied when viable tokens exist, and intermediate values provide probabilistic activation. The default value of 0.0 disables XTC filtering.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description:
A list of tools the model may call.
Use this to provide a list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for.

**When `tools` is specified, `min_tokens` and `response_format` fields are unsupported.**

min_tokens.title: Min Tokens
min_tokens.description:
The minimum number of tokens to generate. Default value is 0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`min_new_tokens`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.min_new_tokens) argument.

**This field is unsupported when `tools` or `response_format` is specified.**

response_format.description:
The enforced format of the model's output.

Note that the content of the output message may be truncated if it exceeds the `max_tokens`. You can check this by verifying that the `finish_reason` of the output message is `length`.

For more detailed information, please refer [here](https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

***Important***
You must explicitly instruct the model to produce the desired output format using a system prompt or user message (e.g., `You are an API generating a valid JSON as output.`).
Otherwise, the model may result in an unending stream of whitespace or other characters.

**This field is unsupported when `tools` is specified.**
**When `response_format` is specified, `min_tokens` field is unsupported.**

input.title: Input
input.description: Text or image inputs to the model, used to generate a response.

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.description: A system (or developer) message inserted into the model's context.

max_output_tokens.title: Max Output Tokens
max_output_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a response, including visible output tokens and reasoning tokens.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or `top_p` but not both.

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with `top_p` probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. We generally recommend altering this or `temperature` but not both.

parallel_tool_calls.title: Parallel Tool Calls
parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to allow the model to run tool calls in parallel.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: An array of tools the model may call while generating a response. You can specify which tool to use by setting the `tool_choice` parameter.

anyOf.description: Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model. `none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message. `auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools. `required` means the model must call one or more tools. An object can be used to force the model to call a specific function or custom tool.

tool_choice.title: Tool Choice
tool_choice.description: How the model should select which tool (or tools) to use when generating a response. See the `tools` parameter to see how to specify which tools the model can call.

text.description: Configuration options for a text response from the model. Can be plain text or structured JSON data.

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the generation result. When set to `true`, the response is sent as [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format) once generated.

model.title: Model
model.description: Code of the model to use. See [available model list](https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/model-apis/pricing#billing-methods).

object.title: Object
object.description: The object type of this resource - always set to `response`.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when this response was created.

error.description: An error object returned when the model fails to generate a Response.

status.title: Status
status.description: The status of the response generation. One of `completed`, `failed`, `in_progress`, `cancelled`, `queued`, or `incomplete`.

output.title: Output
output.description: An array of content items generated by the model.

usage.description: Represents token usage details including input tokens, output tokens, a breakdown of output tokens, and the total tokens used.

incomplete_details.description: Details about why the response is incomplete.

model.title: Model
model.description: Model ID used to generate the response.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: A list of conversation messages ordered from oldest to newest. Must contain at least one item.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate for the assistant response. Must be greater than 0 when provided.

model.title: Model
model.description: Code of the model to use. See [available model list](https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/model-apis/pricing#billing-methods).

anyOf.description: Payload format for the top-level `system` instruction.

system.title: System
system.description: Optional top-level system instruction applied before conversation turns. Supports a plain string or an array of text blocks.

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream the response. When set to `true`, events are sent as [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format) once generated.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Lower values make outputs more deterministic; higher values increase diversity.

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter. The model samples from the smallest token set whose cumulative probability reaches `top_p`.

top_k.title: Top K
top_k.description: Limits sampling to the `k` most likely tokens at each decoding step.

stop_sequences.title: Stop Sequences
stop_sequences.description: Stop strings that terminate generation when matched in output. The matched value is returned in `stop_sequence` when applicable.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Tool definitions available to the model. Use this to allow tool calls with structured arguments.

tool_choice.description: Controls tool-calling behavior (`auto`, `any`, `tool`, `none`) and optional parallel-call behavior.

thinking.description: Controls reasoning behavior with mode (`enabled`, `disabled`, `adaptive`). `enabled` requires `budget_tokens`; `disabled` and `adaptive` must not include it.

output_config.description: Output generation options including effort level and structured output format settings.

cache_control.title: Cache Control
cache_control.description: Compatibility field accepted for request portability. Parsed but not used for generation.

container.title: Container
container.description: Compatibility field accepted for request portability. Parsed but not used for generation.

context_manager.title: Context Manager
context_manager.description: Compatibility field accepted for request portability. Parsed but not used for generation.

inference_geo.title: Inference Geo
inference_geo.description: Compatibility field accepted for request portability. Parsed but not used for generation.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Compatibility field accepted for request portability. Parsed but not used for generation.

service_tier.title: Service Tier
service_tier.description: Compatibility field accepted for request portability. Parsed but not used for generation.

messages.content: Hello, summarize what you can do in one sentence.

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique identifier for this message response.

type.title: Type
type.description: Response object type (`message`).

role.title: Role
role.description: Role of the output message author (`assistant`).

content.title: Content
content.description: Assistant output blocks in generation order.

stop_sequence.title: Stop Sequence
stop_sequence.description: Matched stop string when `stop_reason` is `stop_sequence`; otherwise null.

usage.description: Token usage details for this response.

model.title: Model
model.description: Model used to generate the response.

type.title: Type
type.description: Top-level response type for Messages API errors.

request_id.title: Request Id
request_id.description: Request identifier for debugging and support. It may be omitted in some failure paths.

include_usage.title: Include Usage
include_usage.description:
When set to `true`,
the number of tokens used will be included at the end of the stream result in the form of
`"usage": {"completion_tokens": number, "prompt_tokens": number, "total_tokens": number}`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

ResponseFormat.description:
The enforced format of the model's output.

Note that the content of the output message may be truncated if it exceeds the `max_tokens`. You can check this by verifying that the `finish_reason` of the output message is `length`.

For more detailed information, please refer [here](https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

***Important***
You must explicitly instruct the model to produce the desired output format using a system prompt or user message (e.g., `You are an API generating a valid JSON as output.`).
Otherwise, the model may result in an unending stream of whitespace or other characters.

**When `response_format` is specified, `min_tokens` field is unsupported.**

id.title: Id
id.description: A unique ID of the chat completion.

object.title: Object
object.description: The object type, which is always set to `chat.completion`.

created.title: Created
created.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the generation completed.

model.title: Model
model.description: The model to generate the completion. For dedicated endpoints, it returns the endpoint ID.

type.title: Type
type.description: For function calling, the type is always `function`.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the function to call.

type.title: Type
type.description: For custom tool calling, the type is always `custom`.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom tool to call.

format.description: An object specifying the format that the model must output. Configuring `{ "type": "json_schema" }` enables Structured Outputs. The default format is `{ "type": "text" }`.

effort.title: Effort
effort.description: Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. Currently supported values are `none`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh`, `max`, and `ultracode`. Reducing reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response.

code.title: Code
code.description: The error code for the response.

message.title: Message
message.description: A human-readable description of the error.

input_tokens_details.description: A detailed breakdown of the input tokens.

output_tokens_details.description: A detailed breakdown of the output tokens.

total_tokens.title: Total Tokens
total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens used.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: The reason why the response is incomplete.

role.title: Role
role.description: Author role for this message turn. Use `user` for user input and `assistant` for prior assistant turns.

content.title: Content
content.description: Message payload. Supports plain string shorthand or a typed block array.

type.title: Type
type.description: System block type. Must be `text`.

name.title: Name
name.description: Tool identifier used in model tool-call outputs. Keep this name stable and unique within the request.

type.title: Type
type.description: Tool category. If missing, empty, or `custom`, it is converted to a function tool. Any non-empty value other than `custom` is treated as a server-side tool and skipped.

description.title: Description
description.description: Natural-language description of what the tool does and when to call it.

input_schema.title: Input Schema
input_schema.description: JSON Schema describing tool arguments. The model uses this schema to construct the tool input object.

strict.title: Strict
strict.description: If true, applies stricter schema adherence when generating tool arguments.

type.title: Type
type.description: Tool-calling mode. `auto` lets the model decide, `any` requires at least one tool call, `tool` forces a named tool, and `none` disables tool calls.

name.title: Name
name.description: Tool name to force when `type=tool`. Ignored for other `type` values.

disable_parallel_tool_use.title: Disable Parallel Tool Use
disable_parallel_tool_use.description: If true, restricts the model to at most one tool call at a time. Must not be provided when `type=none`.

type.title: Type
type.description: Reasoning mode. `enabled` forces reasoning, `disabled` suppresses it, and `adaptive` lets the system decide.

budget_tokens.title: Budget Tokens
budget_tokens.description: Reasoning token budget. Required when `type=enabled`; must not be provided when `type=disabled` or `type=adaptive`. If `max_tokens` is set, this must be smaller than `max_tokens`.

effort.title: Effort
effort.description: Relative generation effort level. Higher effort can improve quality on harder prompts at the cost of additional compute.

format.description: Structured output settings. Currently supports only `json_schema`.

input_tokens.title: Input Tokens
input_tokens.description: Number of billed input tokens for this request.

output_tokens.title: Output Tokens
output_tokens.description: Number of billed output tokens generated by the model.

cache_read_input_tokens.title: Cache Read Input Tokens
cache_read_input_tokens.description: Number of input tokens served from cache, when cache reads are applicable.

type.title: Type
type.description: Error category. For HTTP 422 in Messages API, this is `invalid_request_error`.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.description: Token prices for input and output

discount_to_user.title: Discount To User
discount_to_user.description: Fractional discount applied to user-facing pricing

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the messages author.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name for the participant to distinguish between participants with the same role.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the message's author.

anyOf.description:
The content of user message, which is plain text.

For **multi-modal format**, use `object[]` type. Support for non-text input is currently in **Beta**.

The content of user message.

**Multi-modal format** can handle not just text, but also audio, image, and video content, allowing for more complex message structures. Support for non-text input is currently in **Beta**.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name for the participant to distinguish between participants with the same role.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the messages author.

content.title: Content
content.description: The content of assistant message. Required unless `tool_calls` is specified.

reasoning_content.title: Reasoning Content
reasoning_content.description: The intermediate reasoning content of assistant message.

reasoning.title: Reasoning
reasoning.description: The intermediate reasoning content of assistant message. This field is a compatible option for the 'reasoning_content' field.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name for the participant to distinguish between participants with the same role.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the messages author.

content.title: Content
content.description: The content of tool message that contains the result of tool calling.

tool_call_id.title: Tool Call Id
tool_call_id.description: The ID of tool call corresponding to this message.

name.title: Name
name.description: An optional name of the tool call corresponding to this message.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

parameters.title: Parameters
parameters.description:
The parameters the functions accepts, described as a JSON Schema object.
To represent a function with no parameters, use the value `{"type": "object", "properties": {}}`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the response format: `json_schema`

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the response format: `json_object`

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the response format: `regex`

schema.title: Schema
schema.description: The schema of the output. Lookaheads or lookbehinds (e.g., `\a`, `\z`, `^`, `$`, `(?=)`, `(?!)`, `(?<=...)`, `(?<!...)`) are not supported. Group specials (e.g., `\w`, `\W`, `\d`, `\D`, `\s`, `\S`) do not support non-ASCII characters. Unicode escape patterns (e.g., `\N`, `\p`, `\P`) are not supported. Additionally, conditional matching (`(?(`) and back-references can cause inefficiency.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the response format: `text`

index.title: Index
index.description: The index of the choice in the list of generated choices.

finish_reason.title: Finish Reason
finish_reason.description:
Termination condition of the generation. `stop` means the API returned the full chat completions generated by the model without running into any limits.
`length` means the generation exceeded `max_tokens` or the conversation exceeded the max context length.
`tool_calls` means the API has generated tool calls.

prompt_tokens.title: Prompt Tokens
prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt.

completion_tokens.title: Completion Tokens
completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the generated chat completions.

total_tokens.title: Total Tokens
total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used in the request (`prompt_tokens` + `completion_tokens`).

prompt_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of tokens used in the prompt.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or `developer`.

content.title: Content
content.description: Text or image input to the model. Can also contain previous assistant responses.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the message input. Always `message`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the function tool call. Always `function_call`.

call_id.title: Call Id
call_id.description: The unique ID of the function tool call generated by the model.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the function to run.

arguments.title: Arguments
arguments.description: A JSON string of the arguments to pass to the function.

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique ID of the function tool call.

status.title: Status
status.description: The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or `incomplete`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the function tool call output. Always `function_call_output`.

call_id.title: Call Id
call_id.description: The unique ID of the function tool call generated by the model.

output.title: Output
output.description: Text or image output of the function tool call. Can be a JSON string of the output, or an array of content parts (text or image).

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique ID of the function tool call output.

status.title: Status
status.description: The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or `incomplete`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the custom tool call. Always `custom_tool_call`.

call_id.title: Call Id
call_id.description: An identifier used to map this custom tool call to a tool call output.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom tool being called.

input.title: Input
input.description: The input for the custom tool call generated by the model.

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique ID of the custom tool call.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the custom tool call output. Always `custom_tool_call_output`.

call_id.title: Call Id
call_id.description: The call ID, used to map this custom tool call output to a custom tool call.

output.title: Output
output.description: Text or image output of the custom tool call generated by your code. Can be a string of the output, or an array of content parts (text or image).

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique ID of the custom tool call output.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the object. Always `reasoning`.

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique identifier of the reasoning content.

status.title: Status
status.description: The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or `incomplete`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the function tool. Always `function`.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the function to call.

parameters.title: Parameters
parameters.description: A JSON schema object describing the parameters of the function.

strict.title: Strict
strict.description: Whether to enforce strict parameter validation. Default `true`.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the function. Used by the model to determine whether or not to call the function.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the custom tool. Always `custom`.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom tool, used to identify it in tool calls.

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional description of the custom tool, used to provide more context.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the output message. Always `message`.

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique ID of the output message.

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the output message. Always `assistant`.

status.title: Status
status.description: The status of the message. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or `incomplete`.

content.title: Content
content.description: The content of the output message.

cached_tokens.title: Cached Tokens
cached_tokens.description: The number of tokens that were retrieved from the cache.

type.title: Type
type.description: Output format type. Set to `json_schema` to constrain output to a JSON schema.

schema.title: Schema
schema.description: JSON Schema object applied when `type` is `json_schema`.

type.title: Type
type.description: Content block type.

thinking.title: Thinking
thinking.description: Reasoning content returned by the model.

signature.title: Signature
signature.description: Signature associated with the reasoning content.

type.title: Type
type.description: Content block type.

type.title: Type
type.description: Content block type.

id.title: Id
id.description: Identifier of the tool call emitted by the model.

input.title: Input
input.description: Parsed tool argument object generated by the model.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Price per input token (alias of input)

completion.title: Completion
completion.description: Price per output token (alias of output)

input_cache_read.title: Input Cache Read
input_cache_read.description: Price per cached input token read

response_time.title: Response Time
response_time.description: Price per response time in seconds

PricingModel.title: PricingModel
PricingModel.description: Pricing model supporting both token-based and time-based pricing.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of tool call.

schema.title: Schema
schema.description: The schema for the response format, described as a JSON Schema object.

content.title: Content
content.description: A list of message content tokens with log probability information.

content.title: Content
content.description: The contents of the assistant message.

role.title: Role
role.description: Role of the generated message author, in this case `assistant`.

cached_tokens.title: Cached Tokens
cached_tokens.description: Cached tokens present in the prompt.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the object. Always `summary_text`.

text.title: Text
text.description: A summary of the reasoning output from the model so far.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the reasoning text. Always `reasoning_text`.

text.title: Text
text.description: The reasoning text from the model.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `text`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

schema.title: Schema
schema.description: The schema for the response format, described as a JSON Schema object.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to determine how to respond in the format.

strict.title: Strict
strict.description: Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the output. If set to true, the model will always follow the exact schema defined in the `schema` field. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when `strict` is `true`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_object`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the output text. Always `output_text`.

text.title: Text
text.description: The text output from the model.

type.title: Type
type.description: Content block type. Must be `text`.

type.title: Type
type.description: Content block type. Must be `image`.

type.title: Type
type.description: Content block type. Must be `thinking`.

signature.title: Signature
signature.description: Optional signature associated with the reasoning content.

type.title: Type
type.description: Content block type. Must be `tool_use`.

id.title: Id
id.description: Optional tool call identifier. Use this value to link follow-up `tool_result` blocks.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the tool to invoke.

type.title: Type
type.description: Content block type. Must be `tool_result`.

tool_use_id.title: Tool Use Id
tool_use_id.description: Identifier of the related `tool_use` block this result answers.

content.title: Content
content.description: Tool result payload. Can be plain text, a list of objects, or null when no body is returned.

type.title: Type
type.description: Compatibility block type. These blocks are accepted for parsing compatibility and ignored for generation.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the message content.

text.title: Text
text.description: The text content of the message.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the message content.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the message content.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the message content.

arguments.title: Arguments
arguments.description: The arguments of function in JSON schema format to call the function.

logprob.title: Logprob
logprob.description: The log probability of this token.

bytes.title: Bytes
bytes.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

top_logprobs.title: Top Logprobs
top_logprobs.description: List of the most likely tokens and their log probability, at this token position.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the tool.

id.title: Id
id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_text`.

text.title: Text
text.description: The text input to the model.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_image`.

image_url.title: Image Url
image_url.description: The URL of the image to be sent to the model. A fully qualified URL or base64 encoded image in a data URL.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of the refusal. Always `refusal`.

refusal.title: Refusal
refusal.description: The refusal explanation from the model.

logprob.title: Logprob
logprob.description: The log probability of this token.

bytes.title: Bytes
bytes.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

arguments.title: Arguments
arguments.description:
The arguments for calling the function, generated by the model in JSON format.
Ensure to validate these arguments in your code before invoking the function since the model may not always produce valid JSON.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the function to call.

type.title: Type
type.description: Image source type. Must be `base64`.

data.title: Data
data.description: Base64-encoded image bytes (without data URL prefix).

type.title: Type
type.description: Image source type. Must be `url`.

url.title: Url
url.description: Publicly accessible image URL that the service can fetch.
